mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending and driving immediate positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month and housing starts declining below 500,000 units signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal savings expanding at an annualized rate above 3.0 percent during a post-recession recovery phase fuels durable goods consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean difference when RSAFS growth exceeds 3.0 percent during recovery phases compared to baseline periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.5 percent confirms underlying economic momentum, reducing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation with forward returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.8 percent year-over-year, inflation fears trigger central bank hawkishness and higher long-term yields, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses the 2.8% threshold, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls increasing by more than 200,000 while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls indicates robust labor demand, which reinforces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of observed periods, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS movements.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 650,000 units during a late-cycle economic expansion signals credit overheating and imminent monetary tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 65% of months where HOUST exceeds 650,000 during late-cycle phases, or the signal's predictive power disappears when GDP growth slows below 1.5%.
